description: Implement updatePotentials: walk the moved subtree via thread+lastSucc and apply the reduced-cost delta to pi using saturating int64 arithmetic.
commit: feat: add potential update for subtree after pivot
steps: Implement `func (s *state) updatePotentials(entering, joinSide int)`. Use s.reducedCost(entering) to compute rc.
Delta sign: if joinSide == 0 (source-side subtree moved), delta = -rc; else (joinSide == 1, target-side moved), delta = +rc.

Determine the moved subtree root: if joinSide == 0 it is s.arcSource[entering]; else s.arcTarget[entering]. Walk via thread starting at the moved root; bound by s.lastSucc[movedRoot]: loop `u := movedRoot; for { s.pi[u] = addSat(s.pi[u], delta); if u == s.lastSucc[movedRoot] { break }; u = s.thread[u] }`.

Create solver_potentials_test.go with `TestUpdatePotentials_SubtreeOnly`: hand-crafted state literal with 5 nodes, known thread/lastSucc/parent, pi=[0,0,0,0,0], arcSource/arcTarget for a configured entering arc, arcCost chosen so s.reducedCost(entering) == 10. Configure joinSide=1 (target-side moved) where target endpoint's subtree covers exactly nodes {target, target.thread.next}. Call updatePotentials; assert pi for exactly those two nodes equals 10, pi for others equals 0.
Add `TestUpdatePotentials_Saturates`: pi[movedRoot] = math.MaxInt64 - 10, configure delta = +100. Assert pi[movedRoot] == math.MaxInt64 (saturated).
Add `TestUpdatePotentials_ZeroDelta`: configure reduced cost to 0. Assert pi unchanged on all nodes.
acceptance_criteria: Subtree walk uses s.thread and s.lastSucc; no scan of all nodes.
Saturation uses addSat from solver-int64-sat, not raw int64 addition.
